acceptance_criteria.description: TypeScript compiles with no errors after adding orchestrate.ts
runPipeline implements four sequential phases with halting on escalation, and onSpecChange implements version increment with downstream invalidation
acceptance_criteria.rubric: Verify: (1) orchestrate.ts exports runPipeline(specId, config?, existingImplDocs?) implementing four phases: Phase 1 — validate spec via refine(); Phase 2 — decompose spec into impl docs via LLM generation prompt, validate each via refine(); Phase 3 — decompose each impl doc into tasks via LLM generation prompt, validate each via refine(), check cross-task invariants; Phase 4 — run all cross-level invariants (CL-S01 through CL-S04, CL-T01 through CL-T05, CL-F01, CL-F02). (2) Pipeline halts at any phase where escalation occurs per REQ-13. (3) Decomposition calls per-level generation prompt functions from Impl Docs 1/2/3 via claude-cli. (4) After successful decomposition and validation, parent document's JSON is updated with child references and status set to 'decomposed' per REQ-19. (5) onSpecChange(specId) increments version, reverts downstream impl docs to 'draft', sets pending/running execution records to 'abandoned', and passes existing impl docs for incremental re-decomposition per REQ-14. (6) existingImplDocs parameter enables incremental adjustment rather than full regeneration. PASS if all six hold; FAIL if any is missing.
